untranslatable_reason: The Half-Height Target Method requires dynamic calculation of formation height based on identified left peak and lowest low within a lookback period. This requires: (1) identifying the highest high (left peak) within a formation period, (2) identifying the lowest low within that period, (3) calculating height = highest_high - lowest_low, (4) calculating target = lowest_low - (height / 2). The backtest engine does not support dynamic multi-bar pattern identification or arithmetic calculations on historical price extrema within variable lookback windows. The exit condition 'CALCULATED_TARGET' cannot be expressed using only allowed indicators and static values.
translation_notes: Entry condition simplified to 'price breaks below previous low' as a proxy for breakout below formation lowest low. Exit target calculation (lowest_low - formation_height/2) requires indicator support for pattern-aware calculations not available in the allowed indicator set. To implement this signal, the backtest engine would need: (1) lookback window parameter for formation identification, (2) support for donchian-style highest/lowest calculations over custom periods, (3) support for arithmetic expressions in conditions. Conservative approach referenced in exit conditions (Table 17.3 failure rates) cannot be encoded without additional parameters.
